differences.title: 1. DATA MODEL
differences.leftDesc: Stores data as numerical representations for complex relationships and semantic meaning.
differences.rightDesc: Organizes data into predefined tables with fixed schemas and relationships.

differences.title: 2. PRIMARY USE CASE
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for AI applications like recommendation engines, image/text search, and anomaly detection.
differences.rightDesc: Best suited for applications requiring ACID compliance and structured data management.

differences.title: 3. QUERYING
differences.leftDesc: Efficiently finds items similar to a query vector, not exact matches.
differences.rightDesc: Precisely retrieves data based on structured query language and exact conditions.

differences.title: 4. SCALABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Scales well for large numbers of high-dimensional vector data and concurrent queries.
differences.rightDesc: Well-established methods for scaling, but can be complex for massive datasets.

differences.title: 5. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Optimized for rapid retrieval of similar items from vast amounts of vector data.
differences.rightDesc: Excellent for complex data relationships and joins but can be slower for unstructured data.

differences.title: 6. SCHEMA FLEXIBILITY
differences.leftDesc: Less rigid schema requirements, accommodating diverse and evolving vector data.
differences.rightDesc: Requires predefined schemas, ensuring data integrity and consistency.

differences.title: 7. INTEGRATION
differences.leftDesc: Designed to integrate seamlessly with machine learning frameworks and pipelines.
differences.rightDesc: Widely integrated into enterprise applications and business intelligence tools.
